moods.description: You want to feel something deep. Sad stories, real tears, genuine human emotion — the kind of watching that leaves you drained but lighter.

subMoods.label: I want to cry — and feel better after
subMoods.description: A moving story that earns every tear. Not cheap or manipulative — just deeply human. You'll feel emotionally wrung out in the best way possible.

subMoods.label: Sad but beautiful — not depressing
subMoods.description: Melancholic stories that feel more like a bittersweet song than a gut punch. You leave feeling moved, not destroyed. Think quiet longing, not hopeless darkness.

subMoods.label: Grief handled with restraint — no drama
subMoods.description: Loss shown as it actually feels — quiet, heavy, and slow. No big crying scenes, no dramatic music. Just honest, understated stories about people carrying something hard.

moods.description: Something feels deeply wrong, but you can't quite explain why. Psychological discomfort, slow dread, and an atmosphere that sticks with you long after it ends.

subMoods.label: Unsettled but not scared — something feels off
subMoods.description: No jump scares, no gore. Just a growing sense that something is wrong you can't put your finger on. The film gets under your skin quietly and stays there for days.

subMoods.label: Paranoid thriller — tension that builds slowly
subMoods.description: You won't notice how tense you are until you realize you've been holding your breath. Conspiracies, characters you can't fully trust, and a sense that something is about to snap.

subMoods.label: Pure dread — doom that compounds scene by scene
subMoods.description: No explosions, no monsters chasing anyone. Just an unbearable sense of wrongness that gets heavier with every scene. You know it's going to end badly. You can't stop watching.

moods.description: Your heart rate goes up and stays up. Action, danger, horror, and high-stakes situations that keep you locked in from start to finish. Not subtle — intense.

subMoods.label: Non-stop action — fights, chases, explosions
subMoods.description: Full throttle from minute one. You're here for movement, impact, and spectacle. Choreographed fights, insane chases, things blowing up. Your brain rests, your pulse doesn't.

subMoods.label: Survival story — one person against impossible odds
subMoods.description: A character in a situation with no good options. Claustrophobic, relentless, desperate. You're not sure they make it out — and you genuinely care whether they do.

subMoods.label: Classic horror — monsters, jump scares, things in the dark
subMoods.description: You want to scream out loud. Haunted houses, creatures, things that go wrong fast. This is not subtle psychological dread — this is designed to make you flinch, gasp, and check your closet after.

subMoods.label: Heist or scheme — a brilliant plan executed under pressure
subMoods.description: The thrill is mental. A group with a plan so intricate you can barely follow it — and then the payoff when it comes together (or spectacularly falls apart). Fast-talking, tightly plotted, very rewatchable.

subMoods.label: War and combat — brutal, real, no glory
subMoods.description: War shown without a hero soundtrack. The violence is physical and the decisions are impossible. You feel the weight of every scene. Hits completely differently from regular action movies.

moods.description: No heavy themes, no big emotional investment required. Just enjoyable, entertaining content that puts you in a good mood or makes you laugh out loud.

subMoods.label: Pure comedy — just laugh, no effort needed
subMoods.description: Warm, broad, easy humor. You don't need to pay close attention. It works whether you're fully in or half-distracted. Light, funny, and leaves you in a better mood than when you started.

examples.title: What We Do in the Shadows

subMoods.label: Dark comedy — jokes that make you feel a little guilty for laughing
subMoods.description: Smart, subversive humor with a sharp edge. The jokes land harder because they're about something real. You'll laugh, then think about why you laughed. Clever writing, uncomfortable truths.

subMoods.label: Chaotic and high energy — unpredictable from start to finish
subMoods.description: Fast, loud, and all over the place in the best way. You have no idea what happens next and that is the entire point. High energy from the first scene, never slows down.

moods.description: You want to think. Films that challenge how you see the world, play with structure and logic, or explore ideas big enough to discuss for hours afterward.

subMoods.label: Mind-bending — complex structure, multiple layers, needs a second watch
subMoods.description: Films built to be puzzled over. Non-linear timelines, unreliable narrators, ideas that only fully land after the credits. You will want to talk about it immediately after — or watch it again.

subMoods.label: Surreal and dreamlike — images and feelings over plot
subMoods.description: Logic is not the point here. You experience these films more than you understand them. Symbols, textures, and atmosphere matter more than story. Strange, hypnotic, and unlike anything else.

subMoods.label: Philosophical — big questions, no easy answers
subMoods.description: Films built around questions rather than answers. They take their time. They trust you to sit with uncertainty. The real thinking happens after you close the laptop.

moods.description: You are tired, low energy, or just want something gentle in the background. No emotional demands, no plot you have to track. Just warm, easy, and comfortable.

subMoods.label: Background watching — on while you do other things
subMoods.description: You are not giving this your full attention and that is completely fine. Familiar, warm, low-stakes. Something that makes the room feel lived-in without asking anything from you.

subMoods.label: Fall asleep watching — slow, calm, no sudden moments
subMoods.description: Gentle pacing, no loud moments, no cliffhangers. You will not feel like you missed anything important if you drift off. Soothing to watch and even better to fall asleep to.

subMoods.label: Warm and feel-good — everything is going to be okay
subMoods.description: Small stories about good people doing their best. Uplifting without being cheesy. You finish it feeling like the world is a slightly nicer place than you thought.

moods.description: You want to go somewhere. Epic stories, faraway places, unfamiliar cultures, or grand journeys that make your own world feel bigger just by watching.

subMoods.label: Take me somewhere new — a world or culture I know nothing about
subMoods.description: A window into a place, culture, or community that feels completely foreign to you. The best armchair travel — immersive enough that you feel like you have actually been there by the end.

subMoods.label: Epic scale — wars, dynasties, grand journeys
subMoods.description: Stories so large that the world itself feels like a character. Decades of history, enormous casts, battles and empires. You feel the full weight and sweep of time passing.

subMoods.label: Surprise me — something I would never pick myself
subMoods.description: You are tired of your own patterns. You want a recommendation that breaks your habits — something you would scroll past but will absolutely love once you start it. Trust the match.

moods.description: You want to feel fired up. Stories about people who pushed through, built something extraordinary, or changed the world. You will close the laptop ready to do something.

subMoods.label: Leave me feeling fired up and ready to act
subMoods.description: Characters who hustle, grind, and refuse to quit. You finish the film with energy and momentum. Sports stories, startup battles, artists who made it against the odds — all qualify.

subMoods.label: A character I want to be — someone who changes how I see myself
subMoods.description: You walk away with a new role model. Not a superhero — a real person (or believable fictional one) whose way of thinking, choosing, and living makes you want to be better or braver.

examples.title: Portrait of a Lady on Fire

subMoods.label: Obsessive craft — people who gave everything to one thing
subMoods.description: Artists, chefs, musicians, architects — people so consumed by their work it borders on madness. Watching them is exhausting and exhilarating. You will want to create something immediately after.

moods.description: You are watching with someone — or want something about human connection. Romance, friendship, family, and the complicated mess of loving other people.

subMoods.label: Date night — romantic with actual depth, not a basic love story
subMoods.description: Real chemistry, real tension, real longing. Not a predictable romance where you know the ending in the first ten minutes. Something that makes both of you feel something and talk about it after.

subMoods.label: Watching with friends — holds attention even if people are chatting
subMoods.description: Crowd-pleasing without being dumbed down. Engaging enough that people stop chatting when something big happens, relaxed enough that it works even if not everyone is fully watching.

subMoods.label: Family watch — kids are entertained, adults are not bored
subMoods.description: Works on two levels at once. Kids are fully engaged, adults find something genuinely interesting too. Layered storytelling that does not talk down to anyone at the table.

moods.description: You want to feel like a different time. Childhood memories, a specific decade, or the ache of looking back at something you can't return to. Warm, familiar, and bittersweet.

subMoods.label: Feel like a kid again — childhood stories and wonder
subMoods.description: Stories seen through the eyes of a child — full of curiosity, magic, and small things that feel enormous. Whether you are 20 or 50, these take you back to a simpler version of yourself.

subMoods.label: A specific era — 80s, 90s, or 2000s aesthetic and feel
subMoods.description: You want the look, sound, and energy of a specific decade. Fashion, music, technology, references — all intact. It is not just about the story, it is about being transported to that moment in time.

subMoods.label: Bittersweet memory — looking back at something that can't be relived
subMoods.description: The specific ache of time passing. Stories about people who had something beautiful that is now gone — a friendship, a place, a version of themselves. Warm and melancholic at the same time.

moods.description: You want to get obsessed with something real. True crime, hidden conspiracies, investigative journalism — one episode in and you realize it is now 3am and you cannot stop.

subMoods.label: True crime — real cases, real people, no easy answers
subMoods.description: A real crime told in full detail. Who did it, why, and how. You will form opinions, change your mind, and feel unsettled by how close the truth is to ordinary life.

subMoods.label: Conspiracy and cover-ups — something powerful is hiding the truth
subMoods.description: Systems, institutions, and powerful people who did not want this story told. The more you watch, the bigger the picture gets. Infuriating, riveting, and hard to shake.

subMoods.label: Investigative journalism — reporters uncovering something huge
subMoods.description: Journalists following a story that keeps getting bigger and more dangerous. The tension is in the research, the sources, the risk. One of the most compelling narrative structures that exists.

moods.description: The universal ache of becoming who you are. First loves, big mistakes, belonging, and the painful gap between who you were and who you are becoming. Hits at any age.

subMoods.label: First love and innocence — before everything got complicated
subMoods.description: That specific intensity of feelings when everything is new and nothing is held back. Young love, first heartbreak, the version of yourself before you learned to protect yourself.

examples.title: The Perks of Being a Wallflower

subMoods.label: Finding where you belong — outsiders looking for their people
subMoods.description: Characters who don't fit anywhere trying to find a place they do. The relief when they find it, the cost when they don't. Universal across every age, background, and culture.

subMoods.label: Rebellion and self-discovery — breaking free from what was expected
subMoods.description: A person realizing the life mapped out for them is not the life they want. The push against family, society, or their own fear. Messy, honest, and deeply relatable.

moods.description: You want to feel small in the best possible way. The cosmos, the deep ocean, ancient civilizations, or the sheer improbability of life itself. Humbling, beautiful, and quietly overwhelming.

subMoods.label: Space and the cosmos — the universe is incomprehensibly large
subMoods.description: Stories set against the scale of space or time. You finish feeling tiny, temporary, and somehow okay with that. Sci-fi that is about wonder and possibility rather than conflict.

subMoods.label: Nature and earth — life on this planet is extraordinary
subMoods.description: The natural world in all its detail and scale. Creatures, ecosystems, and landscapes that make you realize how little of this planet you have actually seen. Calming and awe-inspiring simultaneously.

subMoods.label: Ancient worlds and civilizations — humans have been here a very long time
subMoods.description: Empires that rose and fell thousands of years ago. Cultures, myths, and discoveries that reframe what it means to be human. History as something enormous and humbling, not a list of dates.

moods.description: It is late, you are alone, and you want something that matches that feeling. Moody, atmospheric, morally grey. Jazz, shadows, cities at night, and characters making bad decisions for understandable reasons.

subMoods.label: Classic noir — black and white, morally grey, everyone is hiding something
subMoods.description: The original mood cinema. Hard-boiled detectives, femmes fatales, rain-soaked streets, and a world where no one is innocent. Stylish, cynical, and endlessly atmospheric.

subMoods.label: Neo-noir — modern crime, moral ambiguity, no clean endings
subMoods.description: Contemporary stories with a noir soul. The city is still corrupt, the characters are still compromised, but the setting is now. Crime that does not glamorize itself. Nobody wins cleanly.

subMoods.label: Just moody and atmospheric — slow, dark, beautifully shot
subMoods.description: The plot is almost beside the point. What matters is the light, the silence, the texture of every scene. Films that feel like being inside a painting at 2am. Best experienced alone.

moods.description: You want to understand the world better. Injustice, power, systems that affect real people — stories that change what you know and make you care about something you did not before.

subMoods.label: Social justice and inequality — stories about systems that fail people
subMoods.description: Racism, poverty, gender, class — the structural forces that shape people's lives in ways they did not choose. Told through specific individuals, not statistics. Uncomfortable and necessary.

examples.title: In This Corner of the World

subMoods.label: Politics and power — how decisions get made and who pays for them
subMoods.description: How governments, corporations, and institutions actually work — not the textbook version. The compromises, the corruption, and the rare moments when someone does the right thing anyway.

subMoods.label: Environmental and existential — the planet and our place on it
subMoods.description: Climate, extinction, technology, and the future of human civilization. Not doom scrolling — but honest, urgent storytelling about what is actually happening and what it means.

examples.title: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
